sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Jack, presented with neck pain after being in a car accident one week ago.
He described the pain as a 7 out of 10, which is not constant but worsens with movement.
The pain is located in the back of his neck and is exacerbated by flexion, extension, rotation, and lateral bending.
He has tried ibuprofen, which provided some relief, but not significantly.
He has also experienced headaches, but they are not ongoing.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of playing football years ago, but no previous issues with his neck.
He did not seek medical attention after the car accident, citing the cost.
Review of systems: The patient has not experienced any hearing or visual disturbances.
Current medications:
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient exhibited positive pain to palpation of the soft tissues of the neck, positive pain with flexion, extension, rotation, and lateral bending of the neck.
Test Results: The results of the x-ray showed no signs of fracture or bony abnormalities.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: I believe the patient has neck strain, likely due to whiplash from the car accident.
Problem list: Neck strain; Whiplash; [NOT MENTIONED]
Plan:
I prescribed Motrin 600mg every 6-8 hours for inflammation.
I also prescribed Flexeril 10mg every 12 hours as needed for muscle relaxation.
I advised the patient to avoid straining his neck and to be conservative with his movements.
I ordered an MRI to rule out any potential nerve impingement or other issues.
I referred the patient to either physical rehab or a chiropractor for further evaluation and treatment once the MRI results are available.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up with the results of the MRI and to discuss further treatment options.
